# aquacropr ![](reference/figures/logo.png)

R tools and wrappers for running FAO AquaCrop in reproducible workflows.

# Overview

AquaCrop is the FAO reference model for simulating crop yield response
to water stress (Steduto et al., 2009; Raes et al., 2009). Its strong
scientific foundation and relatively low data requirements have made it
a reference tool for research, operational studies, and climate impact
assessments worldwide.

Running AquaCrop at scale, however, can be tedious. The model relies on
a file-based workflow — each simulation requires manually preparing a
set of formatted input files for climate, soil, crop, irrigation, and
management parameters, then invoking the executable, and finally parsing
the output files one by one. This becomes a bottleneck as soon as
simulations span multiple sites, years, or scenarios.

**aquacropr** solves this by providing a lightweight R interface that
automates the entire workflow. It handles input file generation, batch
simulation orchestration, and output parsing — all from R, all
scriptable, and all compatible with standard tidyverse and spatial
workflows. The model engine itself is never touched: aquacropr always
delegates computation to the official FAO AquaCrop binary, ensuring full
scientific validity and consistency with published results.

## What you can do with aquacropr

- **Generate input files programmatically** — convert R data frames
  directly into AquaCrop-compatible climate, soil, crop, irrigation, and
  management files, with no manual editing.
- **Run batch simulations at scale** — automate simulations across
  station networks, climate scenarios, planting date calendars, or
  multi-year periods.
- **Parse and tidy outputs** — read AquaCrop result files into
  structured data frames ready for analysis, visualization, or export.
- **Build reproducible workflows** — script the full chain from raw data
  to final results, enabling version control, traceability, and
  collaboration.

## Who it is for

aquacropr is aimed at agronomists, climate scientists, hydrologists, and
data analysts working on crop-water-climate interactions — whether for
regional yield assessments, climate change impact studies, irrigation
optimization, operational crop monitoring, or research and capacity
building.

## Installation

``` r

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("mwaongo/aquacropr")
```

## Getting started

The first step is to set up a dedicated project directory.
[`init_aquacrop()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/init_aquacrop.md)
creates the standard AquaCrop folder structure and downloads the
official FAO binary appropriate for your operating system.

``` r

library(aquacropr)

init_aquacrop(path = "my-project", version = "7.2")
```

This will open by default a new clean RStudio project. Consult
`readme.txt` to understand the directory structure.

The workflow below walks through a complete single-station simulation:
**climate → crop → soil → management → irrigation → onset calendar →
project file → run**. Every step produces one or more AquaCrop-formatted
files directly from R objects, with no manual editing.

### Climate

aquacropr ships with a built-in `weather` dataset containing daily
observations for a single station. Inspect it first, then a single call
to
[`write_climate()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_climate.md)
generates all four climate files required by AquaCrop.

``` r

head(weather)
#>   year month day rain  et0 tmin tmax
#> 1 1976     1   1  0.0 3.21  9.2 24.1
#> 2 1976     1   2  0.0 3.45 10.1 25.3
#> 3 1976     1   3  2.4 2.87  8.7 21.6

write_climate(
  data      = weather,
  site_name = "wakanda"
)
# → CLIMATE/wakanda.PLU  (rainfall)
# → CLIMATE/wakanda.Tnx  (min/max temperature)
# → CLIMATE/wakanda.ETo  (reference evapotranspiration)
# → CLIMATE/wakanda.CLI  (master file linking the three above)
```

If you need finer control over file content or naming, individual
writers
([`write_plu()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_plu.md),
[`write_tnx()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_tnx.md),
[`write_eto()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_eto.md),
[`write_cli()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cli.md))
are also available.

### Crop

[`write_cro()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cro.md)
generates the crop parameter file. Default parameters are provided for
all crops supported by AquaCrop. Individual parameters can be overridden
via `params = list(...)`, where each element is named by its AquaCrop
variable identifier.

``` r

write_cro(
  crop_name = "maize",
  params = list(
    var_35 = 1.10,  # higher crop coefficient
    var_50 = 0.85,  # maximum canopy cover
    var_64 = 35     # reference harvest index
  )
)
# → CROP/maize.CRO
```

### Soil

[`write_sol()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol.md)
generates the soil profile and initial water content from layer-wise
descriptors. Pedotransfer functions (Saxton & Rawls, 2006) are applied
automatically to derive hydraulic properties from texture when measured
values are unavailable. Salinity class and curve number can be specified
per layer.

``` r

write_sol(
  site_name     = "wakanda",
  cn            = c(65, 70),
  rew           = c(7, 5),
  texture       = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),
  thickness     = c(0.4, 0.6),
  initial_water = "WP",
  salinity      = "very slight"
)
# → SOIL/wakanda.SOL
# → SOIL/wakanda.SW0
```

### Management

[`write_man()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_man.md)
generates the field management file. Soil fertility level, mulch cover,
and evaporation reduction from mulch are controlled via `params`.

``` r

write_man(
  path      = "MANAGEMENT/",
  site_name = "wakanda",
  params = list(
    var_05 = 35,  # 35% fertility level
    var_03 = 30,  # 30% mulch cover
    var_04 = 50   # 50% evaporation reduction from mulch
  )
)
# → MANAGEMENT/wakanda.MAN
```

### Irrigation

aquacropr supports all AquaCrop irrigation modes. For automatic
irrigation (mode 2),
[`create_irr_schedule()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/create_irr_schedule.md)
first builds the schedule data frame — defining trigger thresholds and
application depths for successive crop periods — which is then passed to
[`write_irr()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_irr.md).

``` r

schedule_raw <- create_irr_schedule(
  from_day        = c(1, 30, 60),
  time_crit       = c(50, 40, 30),   # % RAW depletion threshold per period
  depth_crit      = c(0, 0, 5),      # back to FC (+ 5 mm on last period)
  ecw             = c(0.5, 0.6, 0.7),
  time_crit_code  = 3,               # criterion: % RAW
  depth_crit_code = 1                # application: back to field capacity
)

write_irr(
  path        = "MANAGEMENT/",
  site_name   = "wakanda",
  method      = 5,
  wet_surface = 30,
  mode        = 2,
  irr_data    = schedule_raw
)
# → MANAGEMENT/wakanda.IRR
```

### Onset calendar

When planting dates depend on seasonal rainfall onset rather than a
fixed calendar date — the standard situation in rainfed systems —
[`write_cal()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cal.md)
defines the onset criterion.
[`find_onset()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/find_onset.md)
is then called internally by
[`write_prm()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm.md)
to derive planting dates for each year in the climate record.

``` r

write_cal(
  site_name     = "wakanda",
  onset         = "rainfall",
  window_start  = 121,   # search starts at DOY 121 (May 1)
  window_length = 92,    # 92-day search window
  criterion     = 4,     # cumulative rainfall criterion
  preset_value  = 50,    # 50 mm threshold
  occurrences   = 1
)
# → CAL/wakanda.CAL
```

### Project file

[`write_prm()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm.md)
assembles all inputs into a single `.PRM` file. When `calendar_path` is
provided,
[`find_onset()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/find_onset.md)
is called internally to derive planting dates from the climate series
and the `.CAL` criterion — no `planting_schedule` argument is needed.
Fixed planting dates can alternatively be supplied directly as a data
frame.

``` r

write_prm(
  site_name       = "wakanda",
  crop_name       = "maize",
  irrigation_path = "MANAGEMENT",
  calendar_path   = "CAL/"
  # or, for fixed dates:
  # planting_schedule = data.frame(year = 1976:2005, planting_doy = 121)
)
# → LIST/wakanda.PRM
```

### Running the simulation

``` r

run_aquacrop()
```

## Reading input files

All climate and calendar input files can be read back into tidy data
frames for inspection or further processing.

``` r

read_climate("CLIMATE/wakanda.CLI")  # master file + data
read_plu("CLIMATE/wakanda.PLU")
read_tnx("CLIMATE/wakanda.Tnx")
read_eto("CLIMATE/wakanda.ETo")      # alias: read_et0()
read_cal("CAL/wakanda.CAL")          # onset parameters
```

## Key functions

| File type | Writer | Batch writer |
|----|----|----|
| Climate (all) | [`write_climate()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_climate.md) | — |
| Rainfall | [`write_plu()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_plu.md) | — |
| Temperature | [`write_tnx()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_tnx.md) | — |
| Reference ET | [`write_eto()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_eto.md) | — |
| Crop | [`write_cro()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cro.md) | — |
| Soil profile | [`write_sol()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol.md) | [`write_sol_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol_batch.md) |
| Management | [`write_man()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_man.md) | [`write_man_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_man_batch.md) |
| Irrigation | [`write_irr()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_irr.md) | `write_irr_batch()` |
| Irr. schedule | [`create_irr_schedule()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/create_irr_schedule.md) | — |
| Calendar (onset) | [`write_cal()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cal.md) | [`write_cal_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cal_batch.md) |
| Project file | [`write_prm()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm.md) | [`write_prm_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm_batch.md) |

# Package index

## All functions

- [`CropData`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/CropData.md)
  : AquaCrop Crop Parameters
- [`ManData`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/ManData.md)
  : AquaCrop Management Parameters
- [`SWOData`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/SWOData.md)
  : Soil Initial Water Content Default Values
- [`SoilWater`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/SoilWater.md)
  : Soil Water Characteristics Based on Soil Texture
- [`build_crop_parameters()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/build_crop_parameters.md)
  [`crop_params()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/build_crop_parameters.md)
  : Build a Crop Parameter Set
- [`build_source()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/build_source.md)
  : Build AquaCrop from Source
- [`calculate_crop_stages()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/calculate_crop_stages.md)
  : Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar
- [`calculate_harvest_index_timing()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/calculate_harvest_index_timing.md)
  : Calculate Harvest Index Building Parameters
- [`calculate_plant_density()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/calculate_plant_density.md)
  [`plant_density()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/calculate_plant_density.md)
  : Calculate Plant Population Density
- [`create_irr_events()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/create_irr_events.md)
  : Create Irrigation Events Data
- [`create_irr_schedule()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/create_irr_schedule.md)
  : Create Irrigation Schedule Rules Data
- [`day_number()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/day_number.md)
  [`DayNumber()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/day_number.md)
  : Compute AquaCrop day-number from a given date
- [`download_source()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/download_source.md)
  : Download AquaCrop Source Code
- [`ece_to_salinity()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/ece_to_salinity.md)
  : Get Salinity Description from ECe Value
- [`find_onset()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/find_onset.md)
  : Compute AquaCrop Growing Season Onset Day per Year
- [`init_aquacrop()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/init_aquacrop.md)
  : Initialize AquaCrop Project Structure
- [`install_binaries()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/install_binaries.md)
  : Install AquaCrop Binary
- [`install_source()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/install_source.md)
  : Install AquaCrop from Source
- [`is_cli()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/is_cli.md)
  : Check if file is a valid AquaCrop climate file
- [`is_eto()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/is_eto.md)
  : Check if file is a valid AquaCrop reference evapotranspiration file
- [`is_leap_year()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/is_leap_year.md)
  : Check if a Year is a Leap Year
- [`is_plu()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/is_plu.md)
  : Check if file is a valid AquaCrop rainfall file
- [`is_tnx()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/is_tnx.md)
  : Check if file is a valid AquaCrop temperature file
- [`params_df2list()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/params_df2list.md)
  : Convert data.frame to parameter list for batch functions
- [`path_to_file()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/path_to_file.md)
  : Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacropr
- [`read_cal()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_cal.md)
  : Parse an AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File
- [`read_cli()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_cli.md)
  [`read_climate()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_cli.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop CLI File
- [`read_day_out()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_day_out.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop day.out file
- [`read_eto()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_eto.md)
  [`read_et0()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_eto.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop ETo File
- [`read_plu()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_plu.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop PLU File
- [`read_season_out()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_season_out.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop Season Output File
- [`read_tnx()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/read_tnx.md)
  : Read an AquaCrop Tnx File
- [`round_to()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/round_to.md)
  : Round to Nearest Multiple
- [`run_aquacrop()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/run_aquacrop.md)
  : Run AquaCrop simulation
- [`salinity_to_ece()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/salinity_to_ece.md)
  : Convert Salinity Level to ECe Value
- [`st_grid()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/st_grid.md)
  : Create Grid from Points
- [`to_aquacrop_day()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/to_aquacrop_day.md)
  : Convert Dates to AquaCrop Day Numbers
- [`weather`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/weather.md)
  : Example Weather Data for AquaCrop
- [`write_cal()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cal.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File
- [`write_cal_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cal_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) Files for Multiple Sites
- [`write_cli()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cli.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Climate File
- [`write_climate()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_climate.md)
  : Write Complete AquaCrop Climate Dataset
- [`write_cro()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_cro.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Crop File
- [`write_eto()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_eto.md)
  [`write_et0()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_eto.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Reference Evapotranspiration File
- [`write_fwf()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_fwf.md)
  : Write Fixed-Width Format File
- [`write_gwt()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_gwt.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File
- [`write_gwt_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_gwt_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) Files for Multiple Stations
- [`write_irr()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_irr.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Irrigation File
- [`write_irrig_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_irrig_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Irrigation (.IRR) Files for Multiple Stations
- [`write_man()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_man.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Management File
- [`write_man_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_man_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop MAN files in batch
- [`write_obs()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_obs.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Observed Data (.OBS) File
- [`write_obs_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_obs_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Observed Data (.OBS) Files for Multiple Stations
- [`write_off()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_off.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Off-Season Conditions (.OFF) File
- [`write_off_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_off_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Off-Season Conditions (.OFF) Files for Multiple
  Stations
- [`write_plu()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_plu.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Rainfall File
- [`write_ppn()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_ppn.md)
  : Write an AquaCrop Program Parameter (.PPn) File
- [`write_prm()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Parameter (.PRM) Files
- [`write_prm_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_prm_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop PRM files for Multiple Stations
- [`write_sim()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sim.md)
  : Write an AquaCrop Simulation Settings File
- [`write_sol()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol.md)
  [`write_soil()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL)
- [`write_sol_batch()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_sol_batch.md)
  : Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch
- [`write_swo()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_swo.md)
  [`write_sw0()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_swo.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File
- [`write_tnx()`](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/reference/write_tnx.md)
  : Write AquaCrop Temperature File

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Regional Crop Simulations Using
  aquacropr](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/articles/regionalsim.md):
- [Getting Started: Initializing an AquaCrop
  Project](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/articles/settingup.md):
- [Onset of the Growing Season: Methods, Limitations, and Sahelian
  Realities](https://mwaongo.github.io/aquacropr/articles/sowingdate.md):
